name: Bashkortostan (Russia)

colors.symbolism: clarity, virtue, and the noble thoughts of the peoples of Bashkortostan
peace, openness, and the willingness to cooperate
the gold of the kurai flower and the wealth of the Bashkir land

description: A horizontal triband of blue, white and green of equal width, with a stylised golden seven-petalled kurai flower in the centre of the white stripe.
history: Adopted on 25 February 1992 by the Supreme Soviet of the Republic of Bashkortostan. The design was created by Urail Masalimov; the kurai emblem and the seven petals are the same motif used on the republic's coat of arms.

features.symbolism: blue stands for clarity, virtue, and good thought of the peoples of the republic; white for peace, openness, and the readiness for cooperation; green for freedom and eternal life.
a stylised kurai (a hogweed-like plant whose stem is used to make the Bashkir national flute); its seven petals represent the seven Bashkir clans who united to form the Bashkir people.
